title: Four wheels
requestCopy: Anything on four wheels that starts every morning. I have almost no money. Please.
deliveredCopy: It starts. You have no idea what that means to me.
overdeliveredCopy: It starts AND the radio works? You're wasted on this town.
lapsedCopy: I found a moped. It's fine. It's not fine.

title: Won't strand her
requestCopy: My daughter drives forty minutes to college. Build me the car that never leaves her on the roadside.
deliveredCopy: I'll sleep now, I think. Thank you.
overdeliveredCopy: You checked things I didn't know cars had. I'm telling the whole neighbourhood.
lapsedCopy: Her uncle lent her his van. I'd rather it had been you.

title: First proper car
requestCopy: I've saved up. I want a PROPER car this time, one I won't be embarrassed by, one that still starts every morning.
deliveredCopy: It's exactly what I meant and I never said what I meant.
overdeliveredCopy: My dad asked to drive it. My DAD.
lapsedCopy: Bought one off a bloke at the station. I already regret it.

title: Make it pull
requestCopy: Customer of mine wants a warm hatch, nothing silly, but it has to PULL. You find the base, you build it.
deliveredCopy: Clean work. It goes like it should.
overdeliveredCopy: You went past the brief. I respect that. So does the dyno.

title: The column clock
requestCopy: I'm writing about the pass again. Bring me something that clocks under the time printed in this column, and I'll put your garage's name in it.
deliveredCopy: Under the mark. The column runs next week; your name's in the last line.
overdeliveredCopy: WELL under. That's a headline, not a footnote.
lapsedCopy: Deadline's gone. The column ran with someone else's time.

title: Low and loud
requestCopy: It needs to sit RIGHT. Low, wide, loud, and it still has to look like it means it when it's parked.
overdeliveredCopy: People will stop walking when they see this. That's the whole point.
lapsedCopy: The meet came and went, mate.

title: Street power, street manners
requestCopy: Real power, but it idles like a street car and my customer drives it to work. Both. No excuses.
deliveredCopy: Power AND manners. That's the hard one, and you did it.
overdeliveredCopy: It made the number and then it just... idled. Beautiful.
lapsedCopy: He bought something off the shelf instead. Shame. It'll break.

title: Under one-fifteen
requestCopy: Last one. The big column. Under one-fifteen at Kirifuri, whatever it takes, and your garage is the name this town remembers.
deliveredCopy: That's the time. That's the story. Go read your own name.
overdeliveredCopy: You didn't beat the time, you erased it. Front page.
lapsedCopy: The season's over. Some stories don't get written.
